FB1a-fileSize.title: `fileSize Long` field on `:ShepardFile` (Mongo + Neo4j round-trip via OGM), captured at upload time from `GridFSBucket.find(...).getLength()`. IX1.title: External-service minimal-instance infrastructure — prefer running **the real service in a minimal Docker container** over synthetic WireMock stubs; stubs only for services with no self-hostable open-source image. Target list and preferred image for each: (a) **Helmholtz Databus + MOSS** (`R1/MB1`) — Apache Jena Fuseki testcontainer (`stain/jena-fuseki`) is the actual SPARQL store Databus uses; MOSS indexer gets a real minimal MOSS module config pointed at it; validates the full SHACL + DCAT round-trip against real SPARQL semantics. (b) **AAS Registry** (`AAS1-reg`) — `eclipsebasyx/aas-environment:2.x` official image; exercises the real BaSyx REST wire shape including `AssetAdministrationShell` registration payloads. (c) **Matrix home server** (`MTX1a`) — `matrixdotorg/synapse:latest` or `conduit` minimal testcontainer; real `/_matrix/client/v3` routes so the notification flow is exercised against actual Matrix protocol. (d) **Open3D sidecar** (`PC1b`, `VID2a`) — real image we build from `infrastructure/sidecar-open3d/`; testcontainered directly via the built image tag. (e) **SA-Python bridge** (`SA1`) — if the bridge is an HTTP microservice we control, testcontainer it directly; SA COM itself is not CI-testable (Windows-only), so the CI test validates the bridge's HTTP contract against a fixture PLY file. (f) **Helmholtz Unhide HKG harvester** (`UH1`) — no official self-hostable harvester image; use a minimal WireMock container (`wiremock/wiremock:3`) that calls back our feed URL and verifies the JSON-LD shape. (g) **DataCite** (`KIP1d`) — no self-hostable Fabrica; CI falls back to `api.test.datacite.org` OR a WireMock stub set for `/dois` CRUD + `/heartbeat`. (h) **ePIC handle service** (`KIP1c`) — no self-hostable image; WireMock stub for `POST /api/handles/{prefix}/{suffix}`. Pattern: `ExternalServiceStack` Quarkus test-resource (`@QuarkusTestResource`) accepts a `services` init-arg; each enum value starts the correct Testcontainer or WireMock map; `infrastructure-local/docker-compose.external-services.yml` exposes the same images for local dev and manual integration runs without test framework overhead.

commits.subject: docs(IX1): external-service minimal-instance test infrastructure

KIP1h.title: Rename `MockMinter` → `LocalMinter` and extract as `plugins/minter-local/` (strict plugin shape per the user's "MockMinter is a plugin" directive + CLAUDE.md heuristic #3). Ship **versioned PIDs Phase 1** — local PID format `shepard:<instance.id>:<kind>:<appId>:v<n>` (was `mock:shepard:<kind>:<appId>:<epoch-millis>`); `:Publication` grows a `versionNumber` property; `?force=true` bumps the version instead of stamping a fresh epoch. V31 backfill stamps `versionNumber=1` on every legacy row.

P21-File-prereq.title: **Architectural finding (P21-File agent stop-and-report):** the entire `data/` container family (`FileRest`, `StructuredDataRest`, `SpatialDataPointRest`, `TimeseriesRest`) has **no `@PUT` for entity metadata** — only `@PUT /{id}/permissions`. None of the `*ContainerService` classes expose an `updateContainer` method. PATCH on those resources is blocked on a prior decision: should data containers be metadata-editable post-creation, or stay create-only-then-delete?

P21-References-prereq.title: **Architectural finding:** the `*ReferenceRest` family (`BasicReference`, `CollectionReference`, `DataObjectReference`, `FileReference`, `SpatialDataReference`, `StructuredDataReference`, `TimeseriesReference`, `URIReference`) has **no `@PUT` for entity metadata** — same shape as P21-File-prereq. References are create-only-then-delete in the current model.

PM1a.title: `PluginManifest` SPI + `PluginRegistry` (`@ApplicationScoped`, observes `StartupEvent` post-migrations) + `backend/plugins/` drop-in directory + Dockerfile mount-point + flip UH1a from source-stitched to drop-in JAR (the first real plugin under the new shape).

PM1b.title: Admin REST + CLI parity for the PM1a `PluginRegistry`: `GET /v2/admin/plugins` (registry snapshot, full state space including DISABLED + FAILED), `PATCH /v2/admin/plugins/{id}` (RFC 7396 merge-patch — only `enabled` patchable; `plugin.config.read-only-field` 400 + `plugin.not-found` 404 RFC 7807 envelopes); `shepard-admin plugins {list,enable,disable}` CLI verbs with shared `--output={human,json}` / `--url` / `--api-key`. `PluginEntry` gains a `registeredAt` timestamp surfaced in the REST shape so operators see discovery order chronologically.

PM1b2.title: `JarSignatureVerifier` for untrusted JARs (four `shepard.plugins.signing.*` config keys, default `required=false`); semver-range enforcement of `shepardCompatibility()` (incompatible plugins land in `FAILED` state with `plugin.compatibility.failed` / `plugin.compatibility.unparseable` failureMessages; `shepard.plugins.compatibility.strict=true` default + operator-override valve); `DEGRADED` lifecycle state + `PluginEntry.markDegraded()` lifecycle hook (forward placeholder for PM1b3 — child-classloader CDI integration deferred to PM1b3 per `aidocs/69`). `VersionRange` parser extended to accept the npm/Composer-style operator-comma syntax (`>=5.2.0,<6`) every existing plugin manifest already uses.

PM1b3.title: True runtime CDI integration — a vendor drops `shepard-plugin-foo-x.y.z.jar` into `/deployments/plugins/` at runtime and its REST endpoints + admin CLI subcommand come up **without** declaring the plugin in the backend's `with-plugins` Maven profile. Option B (per the `aidocs/69` analysis): extend `PluginContext` with `registerHttpRoute(pathPattern, handler)` so plugins ship Vert.x handlers instead of `@Path` resources (side-steps Quarkus's build-time CDI scanning); wire `PluginRegistry` to a singleton `PluginRouter` that manages the live Vert.x route table. PM1b2's `DEGRADED` state becomes the auto-detection signal: a JAR-walk plugin without a matching `quarkus.index-dependency.*` config key lands DEGRADED with `plugin.runtime.no-cdi-scan` and the failureMessage points at the runbook. Ship a vendor-style example plugin (`plugins/example-video/`) that's NOT in the `with-plugins` profile, demonstrating the drop-in path end-to-end.

PM1c.title: Enrich `PluginManifest` SPI with admin-visible metadata (`title()`, `description()`, `homepageUrl()`, `repositoryUrl()`, `licence()`) + plugin-dependency resolution (`dependencies()` returning `List<PluginDependency>`; Maven-style version-range constraints parsed by the in-tree `VersionRange` parser — no `org.apache.maven:maven-artifact` dependency added). Registry topologically sorts the dependency graph at startup via Kahn's algorithm; failure modes (`plugin.dependency.cycle` / `plugin.dependency.missing` / `plugin.dependency.version-mismatch`) mark the affected plugin `FAILED` fail-soft. Wire shape of `GET /v2/admin/plugins` grows six fields (all nullable / arrays — backwards-compatible additive); `shepard-admin plugins list` table grows three columns (TITLE, LICENCE, REPOSITORY, width-capped at 30 / 12 / 40). `UnhidePluginManifest` filled in with title / description / homepage / repository / `Apache-2.0` licence; dependencies left empty (UH1a depends on in-tree SPIs only).

PM1d.title: CLI extensibility SPI (`de.dlr.shepard.cli.plugin.AdminCliCommandProvider`) + `CliPluginBootstrap` ServiceLoader walk of the same plugin directory the backend uses (`SHEPARD_PLUGINS_DIR` / sys-prop `shepard.plugins.dir`); migrate the existing `UnhideCommand` from `cli/src/...` to `plugins/unhide/src/...cli/` as the proof-of-shape. Plugin JARs ship their own Picocli subcommands via a `META-INF/services/de.dlr.shepard.cli.plugin.AdminCliCommandProvider` file pointing at an implementation that returns the {@code @Command}-annotated class. `ShepardAdmin.commandLine()` is the new public entry point that runs the bootstrap before returning — `main` and tests both go through it. CLI's `with-plugins` Maven profile (default-active) copies every `de.dlr.shepard.plugins:*` `provided` dep into `target/plugins/` at packaging time, mirroring the backend's profile shape. CLI's pom also adds a `maven-jar-plugin` `test-jar` execution so plugin modules can pull the shared `StubBackend`/`CliRunner` test fixtures via `<type>test-jar</type>`.

PM1e.title: Persist runtime overrides (`PluginRuntimeOverride` Neo4j entity, `HasAppId`) so a `disable` survives a restart without editing `application.properties`. Was the "persist" slot historically labelled PM1c/PM1d; renumbered to PM1e to keep the chain monotonic after PM1c (manifest enrichment) + PM1d (CLI extensibility) shipped.

PM1f.title: `sidecars()` declaration on `PluginManifest` SPI + file-s3 Garage spec

PR1.title: Process design + runtime in shepard — umbrella (replaces SPW runtime over time)

PROV-V15.1.title: MFFD import v15.1 (snapshot brackets + per-DO AI mode + license + verifier + workers wiring)

PROV1-content-stats.title: `contentCensus` field on `/v2/provenance/stats` — at-query-time entity counts (`dataObjects` / `fileReferences` / `timeseriesReferences` / `structuredDataReferences` / `spatialDataReferences` / `labJournalEntries`) per scope. Drives the casual-user dashboard's "what's in here" tiles next to the activity sparkline. Null for `scope=user`.

PROV1-content-stats-2.title: `byteTotals` field on `/v2/provenance/stats` — at-query-time byte sums by storage kind. v1 ships `fileBytes` (sum of `ShepardFile.fileSize` reachable from the scope). Pre-FB1a rows with `fileSize=null` contribute 0 to the sum — the total is a **lower bound** until those rows are re-uploaded; surfaced as a caveat on the wire schema. Null for `scope=user`. Open-shape: future keys (`timeseriesBytes`, `structuredDataBytes`) get appended without changing schema.

PROV1b.title: Per-entity provenance trail — `GET /v2/provenance/entity/{appId}` + heuristic target-extraction in the capture filter (`TargetEntityResolver` finds the last UUID segment of the request path and maps the preceding plural to a Neo4j label kind)

PROV1c-acl.title: Per-Collection Read-permission gate on `GET /v2/provenance/stats?scope=collection` — non-admins must hold Read on the target Collection (admins bypass; missing Collection → 404)

PROV1c2.title: Pre-aggregated `:ActivityRollup` daily/weekly buckets (only if PROV1c per-request aggregation gets slow on big installs)

PROV1d.title: Frontend per-Collection sparkline dashboard (Vue + vanilla SVG, no Chart.js) — `useFetchProvenanceStats` composable, `ActivitySparklineCard.vue` with header strip (totals + distinct-agent count + per-kind histogram), bucketed-bar sparkline, cumulative-integral overlay, time-range picker (7d / 30d / 90d / 1y). Wired as an `ExpansionPanelItem` on the Collection detail page; backend already enforces Read-permission gate (PROV1c-acl).

PROV1f.title: Nightly retention TTL job (`shepard.provenance.retention-days=730`; `@Scheduled` cron `0 42 3 * * ?`; negative value = keep-forever)

PROV1g.title: PROV-N JSON export (`Accept: application/prov+json`; W3C PROV-JSON subset with `activity` / `agent` / `entity` / `wasAssociatedWith` / `used` / `wasGeneratedBy` blocks)

PROV1h.title: metadata4ing (m4i) content-negotiation on `/v2/provenance/{activities,entity/{appId},count}` — `Accept: application/ld+json` returns plain PROV-O JSON-LD; `Accept: application/ld+json; profile="https://w3id.org/nfdi4ing/metadata4ing/"` (or short `profile=metadata4ing`) returns the m4i flavour with dual `@type: ["m4i:ProcessingStep", "prov:Activity"]`, `m4i:Person` / `m4i:InvestigatedObject` subtype overlays, and `m4i:hasInput` / `m4i:hasOutput` / `m4i:hasMethod` placeholders mapped from the existing PROV-O fields. Unknown profile → RFC 7807 406 `provenance.unsupported-profile`. Plain-JSON callers see no change. Gated on ONT1b (m4i preseed).

T1b.title: `GET /v2/templates` (list; auth'd; `includeRetired` admin-only) + `GET /v2/templates/{appId}` (read; auth'd) + `POST /v2/templates` (mint v1; admin) + `PATCH /v2/templates/{appId}` (copy-on-write: retires prior, mints v+1; admin) + `DELETE /v2/templates/{appId}` (soft-delete via `retired=true`; admin)

T1c-instantiate.title: `POST /v2/collections/{appId}/templates/from/{templateAppId}` — records the `:USES_TEMPLATE` edge (idempotent) and returns the template body for client-side JSON DSL interpretation. Requires Write on Collection; template must be non-retired.

T1e.title: `POST /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/data-objects/from-template/{templateAppId}` — server-side DataObject instantiation from a ShepardTemplate. Requires Write on Collection. Gate order: 401 unauth → 404 collection-missing → 403 no-Write → 404 template-missing → 409 retired → 403 not-in-allow-list → create DataObject → stamp `[:CREATED_FROM_TEMPLATE]` edge → 201. Applies `dataobjects[0].attributes` from template body (Jackson `JsonNode.path()` walk, tolerates missing keys). Name resolution: request body `name` field → `dataobjects[0].name` → template name + millis suffix. Empty allow-list = unrestricted.

commits.subject: feat(T1e): POST /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/data-objects/from-template/{templateAppId}

T1e2.title: Frontend template-driven creation — replace the "blank create" dialogs for Collection and DataObject with a template picker as the **default path**. "Start from blank" becomes a secondary option. Template picker lists all templates allowed for the target Collection (or all templates for root creation). Frontend enforces the T1d allow-list client-side (backend remains the authority).

TS56.title: Replace Apache ECharts with uPlot in all timeseries chart components — `TimeseriesChart.vue`, `TimeseriesAllChannelsChart.vue`, `ChannelPreviewChart.vue`. uPlot is ~45 KB vs ECharts ~500 KB; `setData(data, false)` streaming append eliminates live-mode jank; `Float64Array` columnar model removes GC pressure at 50k+ points. Four-slice migration: TS56a (new `UPlotChart.vue` + adapter + benchmark harness, no production change), TS56b (swap `TimeseriesAllChannelsChart.vue` + live-mode rAF gate + custom tooltip/legend), TS56c (swap `ChannelPreviewChart.vue`), TS56d (delete `TimeseriesChart.vue`, remove `echarts` + `vue-echarts` from bundle).

VID2.title: Camera-to-scene calibration — click-based point-correspondence UI lets a researcher click N paired points (image pixel ↔ CAD/scene world point) to solve the camera extrinsic transform via PnP (OpenCV EPNP+RANSAC; reuses the Open3D sidecar from PC1b). `POST /v2/videos/{videoAppId}/align-to/{cadAppId}` body `{ correspondences: [{imageX, imageY, worldX, worldY, worldZ}], cameraIntrinsics?: [9] }`. User can skip features that are occluded, moving, or unclear — sparse correspondences are fine (min 4, RANSAC-robust above 8). Per-correspondence residual colour feedback (green/yellow/red). Stores `[:CAMERA_ALIGNED_TO { matrix:[16], rmse, correspondenceCount, K:[9], solverAlgorithm, alignedAt }]` on `VideoStream→CadReference`. Unlocks: SB2a CAMERA_FRUSTUM mode (real camera matrix in 3D viewer), 3D annotation → video projection overlay (click annotation → highlight pixel on video), DT1 live camera viewport. Sub-IDs: VID2a (backend PnP + Neo4j edge), VID2b (frontend side-by-side picker UI), VID2c (3D viewer: frustum + projection overlay), VID2d (DT1 camera viewport).
